assumptions: Tesseract OCR confidence and word count are useful legibility proxies, not human usability proof.
Pixel-level contrast and density heuristics should be compared across revisions, not used alone for acceptance.
Estimated Win31 client regions are derived from local foreground pixels and must be compared against live screenshots.

rankedBacklog.acceptance: Every target screen keeps layout bottom margin >= 16px, right margin >= 4px, no console_fit_risk, no log_region_overflow, and the existing DOS-C vision gate passes.
rankedBacklog.finding: 17/17 screens had estimated client fit risk; 17/17 had log-region bottom overflow.
rankedBacklog.hypothesis: Shrink the drawn shell and log/footer region so the whole OPCON client stays inside the 1024x600 proof capture with a visible quiet zone.
rankedBacklog.measurement: Track layout.safeMarginsPx, consoleLog region bounds, region overlaps, and DOS-C vision-gate status on before/after screenshots.
rankedBacklog.title: Make the full Win31 console fit in the capture

rankedBacklog.acceptance: Target views reach median contrast ratio >= 4.5, lowContrastForegroundShare <= 0.25, and mean OCR confidence >= 60 without weakening the existing gate.
rankedBacklog.finding: 17/17 screens had estimated contrast risk; 15/17 had OCR confidence below 60.
rankedBacklog.hypothesis: Use fewer dim foreground colors for body text and reserve low-contrast cyan/amber treatments for decoration or inactive hints.
rankedBacklog.measurement: Track median foreground/background contrast ratio, lowContrastForegroundShare, and OCR confidence by view.
rankedBacklog.title: Raise contrast floor for operational text

rankedBacklog.acceptance: Target screens keep bottomMarginPx >= 16 and rightMarginPx >= 4 while preserving the accepted transcript-first proof path.
rankedBacklog.finding: 17/17 screens had tight bottom or right quiet-zone margins.
rankedBacklog.hypothesis: Give the status/action strip a fixed quiet zone and move transient log lines away from the bottom edge.
rankedBacklog.measurement: Measure bottomMarginPx, rightMarginPx, foregroundRatio, and OCR word count on every captured view.
rankedBacklog.title: Preserve screenshot quiet zones

rankedBacklog.acceptance: Every target view stays gate-passable, decorativeNoiseTokenRatio is below 0.07 on target screens, and no target screen has edgeRatio above 0.105.
rankedBacklog.finding: 6/17 screens produced decorative OCR noise and 17/17 exceeded edge-load thresholds.
rankedBacklog.hypothesis: Keep the retro ANSI style but reserve repeated glyph borders for section boundaries so OCR and human scanning see fewer false words.
rankedBacklog.measurement: Compare edgeRatio, decorativeNoiseTokenRatio, Tesseract meanConfidence, and required view detection before and after a layout revision.
rankedBacklog.title: Reduce ASCII border and decoration density

rankedBacklog.acceptance: The analyzer detects the same plain label for Home, BBS, Downloads, Network, Peers, OTAP, Settings, Wizard, Diagnostics, and Safety in both navigation and page title regions.
rankedBacklog.finding: The baseline includes abbreviated tab/menu labels while view titles and tasks use longer names such as Message Board, Downloads, Diagnostics, and Safety.
rankedBacklog.hypothesis: Recognition improves when the menu, tab, card title, and action label all use the same task name.
rankedBacklog.measurement: OCR each navigation area and compare normalized menu/tab labels against expected task names for every target view.
rankedBacklog.title: Use the same plain task names in menus, tabs, cards, and buttons

rankedBacklog.acceptance: Each high-risk concept has a readable plain-language phrase on the relevant target view and the footer uses those phrases consistently.
rankedBacklog.finding: High-risk terms observed in OCR include OTAP, gate, serial-readonly, PCAP, mesh state.
rankedBacklog.hypothesis: Plain-language helper text should translate OTAP, gate, serial-readonly, PCAP, and mesh state into user-visible operational meaning.
rankedBacklog.measurement: Check OCR for approved phrase pairs such as OTAP -> update request only and serial-readonly -> hardware writes disabled.
rankedBacklog.title: Add novice wording for high-risk concepts

rankedBacklog.acceptance: Each target view exposes one readable view title, one health summary, one primary task region, and one safety/action region in local CV templates.
rankedBacklog.finding: 4/17 screens were text-dense and 0/17 target screens had weak title OCR; 0/17 had tight estimated region spacing.
rankedBacklog.hypothesis: Separate current view, system health, safe actions, and primary task into predictable regions with stronger headings.
rankedBacklog.measurement: Use template matching for heading/control regions, OCR title detection, word count per region, and screenshot bounds checks.
rankedBacklog.title: Make view hierarchy obvious at a glance

rankedBacklog.acceptance: Each disabled control exposes a readable reason and owner path while live hardware mutation remains outside this advisory analyzer.
rankedBacklog.finding: The baseline footer repeats disabled/gated control names; the Safety view has the clearest explanation but the footer remains terse.
rankedBacklog.hypothesis: Disabled Relay, Flash, Serial, and PCAP controls should state why they are disabled and which external path owns the action.
rankedBacklog.measurement: OCR footer/control regions for disabled control name, reason phrase, and owner path phrase.
rankedBacklog.title: Explain disabled controls where they appear

rankedBacklog.acceptance: A later UI-change task includes before/after analyzer JSON, the existing DOS-C vision gate result, ESP32 completion gate result, and no live bench mutation outside the approved lane.
rankedBacklog.finding: The current pass/fail gate is transcript-first; this analyzer adds advisory metrics that can compare later UI revisions without changing acceptance.
rankedBacklog.hypothesis: A stable local JSON/Markdown report lets design revisions improve legibility while preserving the existing completion gate.
rankedBacklog.measurement: Run this analyzer on each copied packet and diff screen metrics, view summaries, and ranked backlog scores.
rankedBacklog.title: Keep a local revision comparison harness
